name: Easing Anxiety
use: Anxious, worried, on-edge, panicky, or overwhelmed by fear and unease.

steps.text: Take a moment to settle into a comfortable position… And as you begin to settle… you might notice that there’s nothing you need to do right now… Nowhere to go… nothing to solve… nothing to work out… Because this time… can simply be a time of allowing… And you may notice…

the way your body is supported… By the chair… or the bed… or whatever is beneath you… And perhaps you begin to realise… that support has been there all along… Even when you weren’t paying attention to it… And as you notice your breathing… You might become curious…

About whether the next breath… will feel just a little deeper… Or simply more comfortable… And it doesn’t matter which happens… Because either way… your body begins to settle… In its own way… at its own pace… And sometimes… people notice that relaxation begins in the body… And sometimes…

it begins in the mind… And sometimes… it begins somewhere in between… And you don’t need to know where it begins for you… Because it’s already beginning… You might bring your awareness to your feet… Noticing any sensations there… Or even the absence of sensation… And as you do… you may find… that something begins to soften… And that softening… can begin to spread… Through your legs… Your hips… Your stomach… And perhaps you notice…

That the more you allow… the more happens… Without effort… Your chest softening… your breathing becoming easier… Your shoulders… beginning to drop… As if they are remembering… What it feels like… to not carry quite so much… And that feeling flows down your arms… Into your hands… All the way to your fingers…

And then gently… up through your neck… Your jaw loosening… Your face softening… Even the small muscles around your eyes… Letting go… And you may notice… That while one part of your mind is listening… Another part… Is already beginning to drift… Deepener – The Path and Letting Go of Control

And in your mind… you may begin to imagine a path… A quiet path… It doesn’t need to be clear at first… Because sometimes… Paths reveal themselves… Only as you begin to walk them… And as you take that first step… You may notice something interesting… That you don’t need to see the whole path…

To take the next step… And that next step… can take you deeper… More settled… More at ease… 10… taking a step… even without knowing everything ahead… 9… allowing the path to unfold… 8… trusting each step as it comes… 7… going deeper… 6… letting go of needing to know…

5… halfway… and already further than before… 4… drifting… 3… softening… 2… almost there… 1… deeply… comfortably… present… And as you continue along this path… You may begin to notice… That anxiety… is not something separate from you… It’s something your mind has been doing… In an attempt to help… Even if it hasn’t always felt that way… And when you begin to see it differently…

Something begins to change… Because anxiety… is energy… Movement… A kind of internal activity… And like all energy… It can shift… It can settle… It can be redirected… And you may begin to realise… That you don’t have to fight it… To change it… Consider this: when a instruments string is pulled too tight, it snaps. When it is too loose, it makes no sound. But when it is balanced… it produces a clear, resonant note.

Your body and mind… like that string… are learning to find balance. Not too tight… not too loose… but tuned to a state where harmony exists. Perhaps your breath is part of this balance. Notice again the in-breath… and the out-breath. Neither is better than the other. Each defines the other. Inhaling would mean nothing without exhaling. And exhaling depends on inhaling. Together, they create rhythm… just as night defines day… and rest defines activity.

And so… anxiety, too, has its place. It exists as a signal… a messenger. But when listened to gently… with balance… its sharpness softens. Like the string tuned just right… your inner world can sound with harmony. And as the path opens… you come to a river… Flowing steadily… Not rushing… Not stuck… Just moving… And you might notice… That the river doesn’t try to control its direction… It follows the shape of the land… And in doing so… It always finds its way… Even when the path bends…

Even when obstacles appear… And you may begin to sense… That there is something in you… Like that river… A deeper intelligence… That knows how to move forward… Even when your conscious mind feels uncertain… And perhaps you don’t need to control everything… To be moving in the right direction…

Because sometimes… Anxiety comes from trying to manage everything… Predict everything… Control everything… And yet… You might begin to notice… That when you loosen that grip… even slightly… Something shifts… Not into chaos… But into flow… And you don’t have to let go all at once… You might let go a little…

or a lot… or just enough… And your mind can decide… What feels right… Just like water… Flowing over stone… It doesn’t force… It doesn’t struggle… And yet… over time… It reshapes even the hardest surfaces… And your calm awareness… Your breathing… Your ability to allow… These are like that water… Working quietly… Gently… Persistently… Even now…

And you may notice something curious… The more you allow a feeling to be there… The less it needs to stay… And you don’t need to make it go away… Because when it’s no longer resisted… It changes… It softens… or moves… or becomes less important… And your mind… Can choose how that happens… Without needing to tell you…

As you continue walking… The path begins to open… And you may notice… That you are walking differently now… More steadily… More calmly… And perhaps without even trying… You’ve become someone… Who trusts the path… Not because everything is certain… But because something deeper… Feels steady… You arrive at a clearing… Open… calm… spacious… And in the centre… A still pool of water… And as you look into it… You may see yourself… Not as you were… But as you are becoming… Calm… balanced… capable… And you may begin to recognise… That this version of you… Has always been there… Waiting… Beneath the surface…

Now, gently bring thumb and finger together… And take a slow breath in… And as you breathe out… Allow that sense of calm… To deepen… And at the same time… That sense of trust… Trust in your body… your mind… your path… And each time you repeat this… It strengthens… Automatically…

And you might imagine… A future situation… Where anxiety once appeared… And notice now… Something is different… You may still feel something… But it doesn’t take over… Because you respond differently… Calmer… More grounded… Allowing things to move… Rather than trying to stop them… And you might even notice… That you trust yourself… Without needing to think about it…

And as all of this settles… You don’t need to make anything happen… Because change… Is already happening… In the background… And just like the river… Just like the path… You are moving forward… Even when you don’t notice it… And perhaps that’s something you can begin to trust… That things are working… Even when they feel uncertain…
And in a moment… I’ll count from 1 to 5… 1… becoming aware again… 2… energy returning gently… 3… body beginning to move… 4… taking a deeper breath… 5… eyes open… calm… clear… and quietly in control…

name: Calming an Overactive Mind
use: Mind feels busy or won't switch off, mental chatter, or too many thoughts at once.

steps.text: Take a moment now to settle into a comfortable position… whether you are sitting or lying down… just allowing yourself to arrive here, in this moment… And as you settle… notice your breathing… notice how it flows in… and how it flows out… You don’t need to change it… you don’t need to control it… simply notice… and as you notice… allow your body to begin softening with each exhale…

With every breath out… imagine releasing just a little more tension… Perhaps in the shoulders… the jaw… the forehead… Breathing in ease… and breathing out release… And as you do this… I’d like you to imagine that you’re standing at the edge of a quiet forest path… The air is fresh… and the ground beneath you is steady and safe…

As you step forward onto this path… each step can guide you deeper into calmness… deeper into ease… One step… two steps… three steps… walking gently, slowly… at your own pace… And with every step… you might notice a sense of quiet growing inside… Almost as if the forest itself is holding you… safe and supported…

Now… as you continue walking down that path… you come to a small clearing… In the clearing, there is a wooden bench… waiting for you… You sit down… and as you do… you notice a soft breeze moving through the trees… This breeze carries with it a gentle invitation… an invitation to let go…

Just as the wind carries leaves away… you too can allow your thoughts to drift… Perhaps you’ve had many thoughts today… perhaps your mind has been busy, spinning, circling… But here, in this place… you can simply allow each thought to become like a leaf… And as each thought arises… see it resting gently on a leaf… and then placed on a stream that flows nearby…

The stream carries it away… gently, easily, without any effort from you… One thought drifts… then another… and another… No need to chase them… no need to hold them… Just letting them float… like leaves on water… carried away, gently, peacefully… And as more thoughts float away… you begin to notice the space that opens up…

A space of calm… of quiet… of stillness… Now, allow that same gentle stillness to flow into your body… Starting at the top of your head… imagine a soft wave of relaxation washing down… Like warm sunlight melting tension… Flowing across your forehead… smoothing it out… Down around your eyes… releasing any tightness…

Over your cheeks and jaw… softening, loosening, letting go… This wave continues down your neck and shoulders… melting away any heaviness… And you may even imagine your shoulders dropping a little more… as if a weight has been lifted… The relaxation moves down your arms… to your elbows… your wrists… your hands… all the way to the fingertips…

And with each breath… the hands become warmer, softer, lighter… The wave continues down your chest… opening, expanding… Your breath becomes deeper, more natural, more effortless… And as it flows into your stomach… imagine it softening, releasing any knots, any tension held there… The relaxation moves through your hips… down your legs… through your knees… to your ankles and feet…

All the way to your toes… Now your whole body is bathing in this gentle wave of ease… Every part of you softening, loosening, relaxing… And now… with your body deeply at ease… your mind can begin to follow… Sometimes, the mind feels like a sky filled with busy clouds… one thought after another, drifting across…

But the truth is… behind those clouds… there is always a vast, open sky… clear, expansive, peaceful… You are that sky… Your thoughts are just clouds… They may come, they may go… but they are not who you are… You are the space that holds them… vast, calm, and steady…

And just as the wind moves the clouds… you can allow your thoughts to drift on their way… Each thought simply a cloud… appearing, then dissolving… Notice the quiet space that remains… And even if another thought appears… you don’t need to chase it… You can watch it… and let it drift away…

As you practice this… you become the calm observer… the peaceful witness… And the busyness of the mind can soften, slow down, and quiet… Now… imagine for a moment that each thought is like a balloon you are holding by a string… Some balloons are small… some are large… some bright, some dull…

But each one represents a thought, a worry, an idea… And in this safe place… you can choose to release them… one by one… See yourself opening your hand… letting go of a string… and watching that balloon float upward… higher and higher… until it drifts out of sight…

And then another… and another… Some balloons may feel heavier than others… but notice how light you feel when you let them go… Notice the freedom in your body… the ease in your breath… Letting go… floating free… And the more you release… the lighter you become… Until at last, you are surrounded only by open sky… clear, peaceful, infinite…

In this space of openness… imagine now that you arrive at a beautiful sanctuary… This place is entirely your own… created by your mind, designed for your comfort… It might be a quiet beach… where waves gently lap against the shore… Or a mountain meadow… filled with soft grass and wildflowers…

Or perhaps a cozy cabin with a warm fire, the sound of crackling wood soothing you… Wherever it is… it feels safe, calm, welcoming… And here in this place… you can rest… deeply rest… All the busyness of the day is outside… far away… Here, there is only stillness… only peace…

Take time to soak in the feeling of being safe, at ease, and completely supported… And as you rest here… you can know that your mind has the ability to be calm whenever you choose… That even when thoughts arise… you can allow them to float by… without needing to grab hold of them…

You can remember that you are not your thoughts… You are the space that holds them… the sky behind the clouds… And in this knowing… you discover freedom… Freedom to rest… freedom to let go… freedom to be at peace… From this moment forward… whenever you wish to return to this calmness… you can do so…

By simply taking a slow, deep breath… and imagining a leaf floating on water… or a balloon drifting upward… And with that image… your mind knows exactly how to soften, how to release, how to quiet… Somewhere within, you begin to feel it:That quiet center—like a still lake beneath a stormy sky.No matter what happens above, the depth remains calm.You begin to remember that this center has always been there. Events come and go…People praise and criticize…Plans succeed or unravel…And yet, this still point within you remains.

Here, you are no longer defined by what happens around you.You respond—but do not react.You move—but you are not pushed. The rhythm of life becomes something you listen to, not resist.No need to grip tightly to any outcome.No need to struggle against what is. From this place, choices become clearer.Action flows not from impulse, but from clarity.There is a quiet strength in you now—Not loud, not forceful, but unshakable.

This strength does not come from control…But from surrendering to what is real. You understand now:Some things change.Some things don’t.And you can easily/naturally tell the difference. You allow space between stimulus and response.A space wide enough for wisdom to arise.A space where you are no longer pulled by every wave,But stand firm…

or bend… as needed. Like the tree that sways but does not fall.Like the current that moves but is never lost. And now… in a moment, I’ll count from one to five… And with each number, you can begin to return gently, slowly, peacefully to the present moment…

Bringing with you all the calm, all the ease, all the clarity you’ve gathered here today… 1… Beginning to bring awareness back to the body… noticing the surface beneath you… 2… Taking a deeper breath… feeling energy beginning to return… 3… Gently moving fingers and toes… slowly awakening the body… 4… Stretching, if you like… feeling refreshed, clear, and at ease… 5… Eyes open when you’re ready… returning fully alert, calm, and peaceful…

name: Letting Go of Overthinking
use: Overthinking, ruminating, replaying things, analysing everything, or stuck in thought loops.

steps.text: Take a moment now… to find a position that feels most comfortable to you… Allow yourself to settle in… Closing the eyes if that feels right… or simply softening the gaze… And begin by noticing the gentle rhythm of your breathing… Air moving in… and moving out… A natural flow… effortless… continuous…

There is nothing you need to change… nothing you need to control… Just noticing… allowing… And with every breath out… you can allow a little more release… Perhaps the shoulders can drop… the hands can soften… the jaw can loosen… As though your body already knows how to let go…

Already knows how to ease itself… simply by being here now… And as you continue breathing… you may imagine that you are standing at the edge of a quiet path… A path that invites you inward… toward a place of calm… a place of stillness… And as you take the first step onto this path… notice how the ground supports you fully… steady beneath your feet…

Each step guiding you gently deeper… deeper into comfort… deeper into ease… One step… then another… and another still… With every step you feel more present… more relaxed… more willing to rest… As you continue along the path… you begin to notice tall trees on either side… Branches swaying in the wind… bending easily… never resisting…

And you might imagine that your thoughts are like those branches… At times pulled this way or that… but always able to return to stillness… Always able to bend without breaking… And perhaps you notice the breeze moving through the leaves… The sound gentle… calming… steady… A reminder that movement and stillness can exist together…

That even as the air stirs… there is peace in the forest… And as you walk further… you arrive at a clearing… In the clearing, there is a place for you to sit… a place that feels welcoming, familiar, safe… You settle into it… and as you do… a deeper relaxation begins to wash over you…

Like soft water flowing down a river… carving its way with ease… Never rushing… never forcing… always finding the way forward… And your mind can begin to follow this same flow… Now… bring your awareness to the top of your head… Imagine a soft wave of relaxation beginning there…

Like warm sunlight gently spreading… melting away tension… It moves across your forehead… smoothing away the lines of thought… Over the temples… down around the eyes… Your eyes can rest now… resting in stillness… That wave moves to the cheeks, the jaw… softening… loosening… Down into the neck and shoulders… melting away the weight you’ve carried…

Perhaps a weight of worries… or endless thinking… And as the shoulders drop… you feel lighter… freer… That gentle wave of ease continues… down the arms, through the elbows, into the hands… The fingers loose, open, at rest… The wave flows through the chest… bringing openness, lightness, ease with every breath…

And into the stomach… softening, releasing… Like untangling knots… creating spaciousness… The wave moves lower, into the hips… the thighs… the knees… Calm spreading into the calves, the ankles, the feet… All the way to the tips of the toes… And now your whole body is surrounded by ease…

Held in a gentle stillness… deeply at rest… And as your body rests… the mind, too, can begin to soften… Sometimes the mind runs like a river in flood… rushing, surging, overflowing… But even the fastest river finds its calm again… The waters settle, becoming clear, reflective, still…

And your mind can settle in the same way… Thoughts may arise… and yet, like ripples, they fade… The surface returns to calmness… The deeper waters remain quiet… steady… untouched… And you may notice that beneath the movement of thoughts… there is always a stillness waiting… Like the calm depths of a lake, hidden beneath the surface waves…

That stillness is here now… It belongs to you… It is who you are beneath the noise of thinking… And perhaps now… you may imagine that your thoughts are like lanterns you have been holding… Each lantern carrying a worry… a memory… a plan… And in this moment, you find yourself in a quiet garden at night…

The sky above is vast, full of stars… And as you hold each lantern… you notice you can release it… Letting it drift upward, softly rising into the sky… The flame glowing gently until it becomes a tiny speck… and disappears… One by one, lanterns rise and vanish into the night…All your worries, all your limiting beliefs, all your self citicisms…imagine each one rising up into the sky…even if you don’t consciously recognise what each lantern represents, your unconscious already knows.

The garden grows darker, calmer, quieter… Until only the stars remain… steady, peaceful, eternal… And you rest there, free of weight, free of burdens… Or if you prefer… imagine thoughts as ropes you’ve been holding tightly… The harder you pull, the tighter they become… And yet, by loosening your grip… the tension disappears…

By yielding… you are free… By softening… you are stronger… By letting go… you discover ease… You may notice that life itself moves in pairs… Light and dark… sound and silence… effort and rest… And just as night gives way to morning… and morning to night again… So too do busy moments give way to stillness…

No state lasts forever… Everything turns, changes, balances… Even the hardest stone is shaped by the softest water… Even the strongest storm yields to quiet skies… And in the same way, your busy mind can soften into peace… Your stress can dissolve into calm… For it is in gentleness that strength is found…

It is in stillness that clarity is revealed… And you are learning, here and now, that calmness is your natural state… Always available… always present beneath the noise… Now… imagine that you step into a sanctuary deep within yourself… This place may take the form of a meadow… a garden… a quiet room…

Wherever it appears, it is yours alone… A place where nothing is demanded of you… Nothing is expected of you… Where you can simply be… Here, time slows… thoughts dissolve… Here, you remember what it feels like to rest deeply… To be soft and strong at the same time…

To be clear without effort… And as you rest in this sanctuary… you know you can return whenever you wish… Simply by breathing… and allowing the mind to soften… From this moment forward… you may find that your thoughts no longer cling so tightly… They may still arise… but they drift more easily…

Like leaves on water, moving with the current… You no longer need to chase them… You no longer need to fight them… Instead, you allow them to pass… And the more you allow… the more peace you find… And each time you soften, yield, release… you discover strength in calmness…

A strength that does not force… does not push… But flows, adapts, and remains steady… This strength is yours… This calm is yours… And you carry it with you into every moment of your life… In a moment, I’ll count from one to five… With each number, you can begin returning to the present…

Bringing with you all the ease, all the calm, all the clarity gathered here today… 1… Becoming aware of your body once again… 2… Feeling energy slowly returning… 3… Taking a deeper breath… 4… Moving fingers and toes… stretching gently… 5… Eyes open when you’re ready… calm, clear, refreshed, at ease…

name: Deep Sleep & Stress Release
use: Trouble sleeping, winding down at night, or wanting to release the day's stress and drift into rest.

steps.text: As you settle into your space now… perhaps lying comfortably, perhaps already noticing the gentle weight of your body resting against the surface beneath you… I invite you to allow your eyes to soften… and if they haven’t already closed, they may soon find it easier… more natural… to close.

And as they do… you may notice that your breathing begins to slow… a little more steady… a little more even… and with each breath out, a soft release, as though the body is letting go of something it no longer needs to hold. You might even imagine the breath as a gentle wave on a quiet shore… moving in… flowing out… without effort… without hurry… just the natural rhythm of your own life moving through you.

And perhaps now… you can sense the way your body begins to melt downward… like a heavy stone sinking into warm sand… perfectly supported… perfectly held… with nothing at all for you to do. With every sound you hear… with every word I speak… you can drift deeper inside… into that soft, spacious place within…

A place where thoughts may float by like clouds in the wide sky… never needing to be chased… never needing to be grasped… just passing… just changing… always dissolving into space again. And as the mind allows those thoughts to drift away… the body can follow… releasing tension here, loosening tightness there, as if all of you were soft clay being shaped by gentle hands of rest.

Notice the way the shoulders begin to let go… as though a great weight has finally been set down… the way the arms grow heavier, softer… the hands warm and relaxed… And as the chest rises and falls… perhaps you imagine each breath carrying away fragments of stress, like dry leaves carried off by the wind. They scatter into nothingness… until the air is clear again… calm again… still again.

And deeper still now… as the legs release into the mattress or the couch… the muscles of the thighs, the calves… as though they too were flowing water… softening, loosening, moving into ease. And perhaps now… your mind can imagine a place… a place of rest, of ease, of simplicity.

It might appear as a valley at dusk… where the air is cool and gentle, and the earth has released the heat of the day. It might appear as a quiet river… sliding silently through stones and trees… its surface so smooth it reflects the silver of the moon.

It might appear as a wide field, open and endless… where tall grasses sway with the smallest breath of air… always bending, never breaking… yielding with grace, and always returning upright again. And whatever image arises for you… whether it comes as picture, or feeling, or only a sense of knowing… let it be the place where your whole self may rest tonight.

This place belongs to you… it is created for you… and in this place, nothing is asked of you, nothing is required of you, except to simply be… to drift… to soften… to rest. And in this place… you might notice something remarkable… that the more you let go, the more supported you feel.

Like a branch that bends in the wind… the strength is not in resisting, but in yielding, in flowing, in being part of the movement itself. And so now… whatever thoughts of the day may linger… they can simply drift like petals on a stream… resting for a moment at the surface, before being carried away… further and further… until they are no longer part of your awareness.

Perhaps you even notice that your body has its own quiet wisdom… it knows how to sleep, how to release, how to renew itself. And you do not need to force it. Just as the river does not force itself to flow, and the clouds do not force themselves to drift, your body too… moves naturally into its own rhythm of peace.

And all you need to do… is allow. Simply allow. And as you rest now… you may notice that time begins to blur… moments stretching out like golden threads… woven together into one continuous fabric of calm. You might even sense your body becoming lighter in some places… and heavier in others… as though you are both sinking and floating at the same time.

This is the sign of deep rest… the sign that your mind and body are harmonizing together… preparing for the healing rhythm of sleep. And as you prepare to drift… perhaps you imagine yourself lying on the bank of that quiet river… the stars above glimmering like soft lanterns in the night sky… the moon shining, silent, steady… and the gentle sound of water carrying away the last traces of your waking day.

You might notice how safe it feels… how natural it is to simply close your eyes to the night… trusting that all is well… trusting that life continues to flow even as you sleep. And the more you trust… the deeper you drift. Tonight… your sleep can be effortless, like the tide that comes and goes… your breath steady, your heart calm… every cell of your body soaking in rest, as a field soaks in the quiet rain.

You may find that dreams, if they come, are gentle… supportive… like friendly guides leading you further into renewal. And while you rest, your mind can unwind its knots, your body can restore its balance, and the weight of the day can dissolve completely. So that in the morning… you may rise refreshed… clear… calm… as though the night itself had washed you clean of strain.

But for now… there is nothing to do, nothing to change, only to drift… to float… to surrender to the deep current of sleep. And as my words continue… they may begin to feel more distant… like voices carried away on the breeze… becoming less important… less necessary…

And what matters most now… is the silence that grows between them… the silence that invites your mind to quiet… and your body to rest. Every pause… every silence… is a doorway deeper… into that place of perfect stillness, perfect safety, perfect sleep. And you may even imagine now… yourself stepping into a soft pool of night… the water warm, inviting, gentle… and as you sink into it… the pool holds you, carries you, cradles you… and you drift effortlessly into dream.

And soon… there will be nothing left to listen to… nothing left to follow… only the rhythm of your own breath… slower now… deeper now… like the ocean at night… vast, endless, calm. And as you surrender completely… you may rest knowing that your body is safe, your mind is safe, and you are free to let go…

Let go into silence… let go into darkness… let go into sleep. And sleep will hold you… as the earth holds the seed, as the ocean holds the drop, as the night holds the stars. Always safe. Always supported. Always resting in peace.

name: Switching Off After Work
use: Can't stop thinking about work, struggling to relax after the workday, or work follows you home.

steps.text: Take a moment now… To simply settle. And you don’t have to try to relax. In fact… sometimes the more a person tries to relax… the more they notice how alert they still are. So there’s no need to try. Just allow yourself to sit… or lie… in whatever way feels natural enough.

And perhaps you can begin by noticing the rhythm of your breathing. Not changing it. Just noticing. Because breathing happens whether you think about it or not… and it continues whether you’re in work mode… or home mode… or somewhere in between. And that’s interesting… isn’t it? How the body already knows how to shift…

without effort. You might notice the air moving in… And the air moving out… And perhaps you become aware of the subtle pause at the end of each breath… that tiny gap… where nothing needs to be done. That gap… is already a kind of switching off. A small example…

of how the nervous system knows how to reset. And as you continue breathing… you may begin to notice sounds around you… distant sounds… nearer sounds… And instead of distracting you… those sounds can simply remind you… that you don’t need to respond to everything. Some sounds matter.

Some don’t. And your mind is already capable of deciding… what requires action… and what can be left alone. And that’s useful. Because for a long time now… your system has been trained to respond. To emails. To conversations. To demands. To problems. Work mode is a state of responsiveness.

Alertness. Scanning. It’s not wrong. It’s not broken. It’s actually a strength. The ability to stay switched on… to notice what needs doing… to anticipate… to solve… That has served you well. And yet… every strength needs its opposite. Just as day needs night. Just as inhaling is balanced by exhaling.

There is a part of you that knows how to stand down. And sometimes that part simply needs permission. So as you sit here… doing nothing in particular… you might begin to imagine that your mind has different rooms. There is a “work room.” And that room contains plans, tasks, conversations, unfinished loops, strategies.

When you are at work… that room is brightly lit. Screens on. Phones ringing. Whiteboards full. And when the day ends… you walk out of the building. But sometimes… the lights in that room stay on. The screens keep glowing. And part of you remains standing there… just in case.

And it makes sense. Because responsible people don’t just abandon things. But what if switching off doesn’t mean abandoning? What if it simply means closing the door… knowing you have the key? Imagine now… walking back into that work room… just for a moment. Notice what it looks like.

Notice what’s active. And then imagine yourself calmly, deliberately, walking around the room. Turning off one screen. Then another. Closing down a laptop. Tidying a desk. Not in a rush. Just systematically. You don’t destroy anything. You don’t delete anything important. You simply power it down. And perhaps you notice how different the room feels…

as the lights dim. And when you step outside… you gently close the door. You might even imagine placing your hand on the door… and saying internally, “That’s enough for today.” Because it is enough. Work is a container. It has edges. And now… you can imagine walking down a corridor…

toward a different room. A home room. A quiet room. This room has softer lighting. Different sounds. Different expectations. In this room… you are not required to perform. You are not required to solve. You are not required to anticipate. You are allowed to simply be. And as you imagine stepping into that space…

you might notice a subtle shift in your body. Maybe your shoulders drop a fraction. Maybe your jaw loosens slightly. Maybe the breath deepens… all by itself. Because the nervous system responds to meaning. And when the meaning changes… the state changes. You see… being in work mode is largely a pattern of the nervous system.

Alert. Ready. Mobilised. And relaxation is not laziness. It is recalibration. When a car engine has been running at high speed… it doesn’t instantly stop. It idles. And then gradually cools. Your system is similar. So instead of demanding instant calm… you might simply allow yourself to idle.

Notice the energy that is still there… without judging it. Because even that energy… can soften… when it’s not resisted. And you might begin to observe the difference between tension and readiness. Tension is gripping. Readiness is flexible. And perhaps right now… you can experiment… with softening just 5%.

Not collapsing. Not switching off completely. Just 5%. As if a dial inside you… moves slightly from “high alert” toward “balanced.” And you may discover… that nothing bad happens when that dial shifts. In fact… clarity often improves. Reactivity decreases. Because reactivity is often just leftover momentum. Like a fan that continues spinning…

after the switch has been turned off. You don’t need to grab the blades to stop it. You simply allow it to slow. And as you sit here… you might bring to mind a recent moment after work… when you noticed yourself still in work mode. Perhaps reacting quickly.

Speaking sharply. Feeling restless. And instead of criticising that… just observe it. That was a system doing what it had practised. But now imagine replaying that same moment… with one small difference. Before speaking… there is one breath. Before reacting… there is one pause. Before answering… there is one inner question: “Is this work…

or is this home?” And notice how powerful that question is. Because when the brain categorises a situation differently… behaviour shifts. You are not at work. You are at home. And home does not require the same speed. In fact… speed can be replaced with presence. Imagine coming home after work…

and before engaging with anyone… you take two minutes. Two deliberate minutes. Perhaps sitting in the car. Or standing at the doorway. And in those two minutes… you imagine physically taking off an invisible jacket. The work jacket. It might be heavy. Structured. Tight across the shoulders. And you remove it.

You hang it up. And you step inside lighter. That simple ritual… can train the nervous system. Because the brain loves patterns. And when you repeat a pattern… it becomes automatic. Just as work mode became automatic. Now you are building a different automatic response. The “switch off” response.

And the more you practise it… the easier it becomes. Like strengthening a muscle that was always there… but rarely used. You might also consider this… At work… you are valued for doing. At home… you are valued for being. Doing and being are different states. And when you allow yourself to fully be…

without agenda… something remarkable happens. The mind reorganises itself. Problems solve themselves more efficiently after rest. Creativity improves after recovery. So relaxation is not the opposite of productivity. It supports it. And as that idea settles… you may notice an even deeper layer of relaxation beginning. Not forced.

Just emerging. Because the mind no longer needs to defend alertness. It knows there is a time to be on. And a time to be off. And both are appropriate. Imagine now a dimmer switch inside your chest. During work hours… it’s turned up. Bright. Sharp. Focused. And each evening…

at a certain time… you reach for that switch. And you gently slide it down. Not to darkness. Just to warmth. Soft light. And you might even decide now… what time that dimming begins. A consistent moment. A boundary. Because boundaries protect energy. And energy managed wisely… feels calm.

As you continue to relax… you may notice that your thoughts are already slowing. Gaps between them increasing. Just like the pause between breaths. And in those gaps… there is peace. Not dramatic. Just steady. And perhaps you realise something important. You are not your work mode. You are the one who can enter it…

and leave it. You are larger than the role. And roles can be put down. Just as an actor leaves the stage… and becomes himself again. You step out of the role… and return to yourself. And yourself… is calmer than you remembered. Stronger than you realised. Capable of quiet.

So from this day forward… each time work ends… a small internal signal begins. It might be the feeling of loosening your tie… or switching off a computer… or locking a door. And that signal means: Stand down. And your nervous system listens. Each evening it becomes easier.

Each pause becomes longer. Each breath becomes deeper. And you may even find that people around you notice. A steadiness. A warmth. A reduced sharpness. Because you are no longer carrying the office into the living room. You are present. And presence… feels like calm. So take a moment now…

to imagine yourself one week from today. Finishing work. Going through your small ritual. Closing the door to the work room. Dimming the internal light. Taking off the jacket. And walking into your evening. Notice your posture. Notice your tone of voice. Notice the expression on your face.

And notice how it feels… to know you can switch off. Because you can. It is a skill. And you are learning it now. Not by force. But by repetition. By permission. By understanding that alertness is useful… and rest is essential. And the more completely you rest…

the more effectively you will work. So for now… just allow yourself to drift a little deeper into that comfortable state. Let the breath settle. Let the muscles soften. Let the mind idle. Nothing to solve. Nowhere to be. And as you prepare to return to full awareness shortly…

you can carry this knowing with you: There is a time to respond. And there is a time to release. And you decide which room you are in. In a moment I’ll count from one up to five. And as I do… you can bring back with you this growing ability to switch off…

deliberately… easily… naturally. One… becoming aware of your body. Two… bringing back gentle energy. Three… carrying calm clarity with you. Four… breathing a little deeper. Five… eyes open when ready… feeling balanced, steady, and fully present. And work… can wait until tomorrow.

name: Stress & Low Mood
use: Stressed, low or flat mood, feeling down, or emotionally drained.

steps.text: Take a moment now… to simply sit or lie back in comfort. And as you settle, notice the weight of your body being carried, supported, held… so that you don’t have to hold anything up, or push anything away. Let your breath be your gentle guide. You don’t need to change it— only notice…

how with each inhale, there’s a gathering… and with each exhale, there’s a softening. With every breath, the body loosens… with every exhale, something unnecessary drifts away. Perhaps you can imagine now… a gentle stream, moving through a quiet valley. The water does not hurry, and yet it always arrives.

And with each breath, you are like that stream… steady, flowing, finding the easy way forward. And as your mind follows the rhythm of this stream, thoughts may come and go, like small leaves drifting on the surface. Some pass quickly, some linger, yet the stream does not hold them.

It allows them to move along, without resistance. With every breath now, your body is becoming more at ease. Muscles releasing. Shoulders softening. The space behind your eyes becoming clearer and lighter. And as you relax more deeply, your inner world begins to open… like the evening sky opening to stars.

Quiet, infinite, welcoming. Imagine now that you are walking through a quiet landscape. The path beneath your feet is smooth and cool. The air around you carries a calm hush… as though the whole world is taking a long, slow breath with you. You notice a gentle wind brushing your skin.

Soft, yet guiding. Invisible, and yet powerful. It moves without effort, and in its movement, there is balance. As you walk, you notice a grove of tall trees ahead. Their branches stretch toward the light, yet their roots sink deeply into the earth. Each tree sways gently, bending with the wind instead of resisting it.

Strong, because they are flexible. Enduring, because they do not fight what comes. As you watch these trees, perhaps you begin to feel something shift inside you. A remembering. That you, too, are rooted… and you, too, can bend without breaking. Allow this truth to settle into your body now.

With each breath, you grow steadier. With each exhale, you feel lighter. With each pause between breaths, you rest in a space of calm and quiet. Now, in this state of calm awareness, your mind is open to new ways of being. New ways of meeting stress, of holding emotion.

Imagine that stress is like a tight knot in a rope. When you pull at it, tug at it, resist it… the knot becomes smaller, tighter, harder to move. But when you loosen your grip, when you soften, the rope relaxes, and the knot begins to untangle itself.

In this way, stress untangles not through force, but through allowing. And so, when stress arises in your daily life, you may remember this. That every breath offers you the chance to soften, to release, to let the knot unwind naturally. Your emotions, too, are like waves upon the sea.

When the storm comes, the surface churns… yet deep beneath, the water is still. And as you rest here, you are learning how to return to that depth inside yourself, where calm always remains. You can let the waves rise and fall. They do not need to be controlled.

They simply need space to move through. And as they pass, the deeper waters hold you in steady calm. Notice how good it feels to remember this now… that even in times of stress, there is always a place of quiet within you, a place untouched by the surface storms.

And when you return to that place, your mood, your mind, your body… all begin to align with balance once again. Imagine now that you are holding a small lantern. At first, its light is gentle, soft, just enough to see the path ahead. But as you walk, the lantern grows brighter, steadier.

The more you relax, the more its light expands. This light is your calm. This light is your strength. And no matter where you go, you carry it with you. Even in darkness, the lantern shines. And the darker the night, the more powerful its glow. Notice how it feels…

to carry this light with you now. Safe. Guided. Balanced. And as this lantern glows brighter, imagine it shining into the places within you where stress once lived, where heaviness once lingered. The light fills those spaces, transforming tension into ease, worry into clarity, fatigue into renewal. You don’t have to force anything.

Just by holding the lantern, balance returns, softly and naturally. Now, I invite you to picture a single feather, resting on the palm of your hand. So light, so delicate, that even the faintest breath can carry it away. And as you look at this feather, imagine that it holds all the stress you no longer need.

Every worry, every burden, every unnecessary thought. All gathered gently in this feather. Take a slow breath in… and as you exhale, release the feather into the air. Watch as it drifts upward… carried by the breeze… further and further away… until it disappears into the sky. Notice the lightness that remains in your hand…

and in your body. A space has opened, a space for peace, for clarity, for calm. And in that open space, new energy begins to flow. Gentle, steady, life-giving. Like fresh water moving into a quiet pond. With every breath now, this energy renews you. It clears away the old…

and nourishes the new. And now, as you rest here, notice how balance naturally arises. Like the day that follows the night, like the seed that splits open to grow. Tension dissolves into relaxation. Worry dissolves into perspective. And every emotion you carry finds its natural balance. When you feel sadness, there is also space for hope.

When you feel anger, there is also space for calm. When you feel stress, there is also space for relief. You are not bound by any single feeling. You are the whole sky, and your emotions are simply passing clouds. Some light, some heavy, all moving on. And the sky remains.

As you remember this, your mood steadies. Not fixed in one place, but balanced, like a dancer poised between steps. And this balance grows stronger each day. In moments of challenge, you return to the breath. In moments of heaviness, you return to the lantern’s light. In moments of stress, you return to the depth of the sea within you.

Always steady, always whole, always complete. Imagine now that some days have passed. Perhaps weeks, perhaps months. And you are moving through your life with a new sense of ease. Notice how you handle challenges now. The things that once weighed you down are now lighter, softer, easier to release.

When you wake in the morning, your first breath feels fresh, clear, open. When you go about your day, your body feels steady, relaxed, grounded. And when stress appears, you smile softly, remembering the stream, the trees, the lantern, the feather, the deep waters within. You know how to return to balance.

And each time you do, it becomes easier, more natural. This is who you are becoming— calm, steady, strong through softness. Carrying light, even when the world grows dark. And as you imagine this future self, notice how it feels in your body now. A sense of confidence.

A sense of peace. A sense of balance that is already here, already growing within you. And so, as we prepare to return from this journey, you may take a moment to thank yourself for allowing this time, this healing, this renewal. Know that what you have experienced here remains with you.

Like the lantern, you carry it wherever you go. In a moment, I will count from 1 up to 10. With each number, you’ll feel yourself returning, bringing back with you a sense of calm, balance, and lightness. 1… slowly coming back. 2… feeling your breath deepen. 3…

becoming more aware of your body. 4… energy gently returning. 5… your mind clear and refreshed. 6… calm, steady, balanced. 7… moving fingers and toes. 8… stretching gently if you wish. 9… feeling lighter, brighter, more awake. 10… eyes open, fully alert, carrying with you a deep sense of calm and renewal.

name: Thinking More Positive Thoughts
use: Stuck in negative thinking, self-criticism, pessimism, or wanting a more positive outlook.

steps.text: You might already be aware… that simply by pausing… something has already begun to change. Not because you’re trying. Not because you’re forcing. But because when you stop… and listen inward… the mind naturally begins to soften. And perhaps you can notice… how your body is already responding.

The way the chair or surface supports you… without you needing to do anything. The way your breathing continues… without effort… without instruction. And that can be interesting… because the body already knows how to relax. It has done it many times before. Maybe just before falling asleep…

Or when daydreaming… Or when you suddenly realise you’ve been staring into space… and time seemed to drift. And you don’t have to try to relax here. Trying… is effort. And effort… isn’t needed. Instead… you can simply allow. And you might notice… which happens first. Does the body soften…

Or does the mind drift… Some people notice their shoulders release. Some feel the jaw loosen. Some notice the eyes resting more deeply in their sockets. And none of that matters. Because once one thing begins to relax… Other things naturally follow. Like a row of dominoes… gently tipping…

one after another. And while that’s happening… you don’t need to concentrate. In fact… the less you try to concentrate… the easier this becomes. Because the unconscious mind doesn’t respond to effort. It responds to permission. And permission… is what you are giving now. You might begin to notice now… that your breathing has its own rhythm. Not too fast. Not too slow. Just right. And you don’t have to change it. Just noticing… is enough. And with each breath out… You may find yourself sinking… just a little more. Not physically…

But mentally. As if your awareness is gently drifting downward… Like a leaf floating on water… Like a feather settling on still air. And perhaps you can imagine… that you are at the top of a gentle staircase. Not a steep one. Not a long one. Just a comfortable one.

And with each number I mention… You can drift down… One step at a time… Ten… stepping down… feeling supported… Nine… sinking gently… comfortably… Eight… letting go of any need to do anything… Seven… noticing that the mind is becoming quieter… Six… thoughts beginning to space themselves out… Five… halfway down… already deeper… Four… feeling heavier… or lighter… either is fine… Three… more distant from the outside world…

Two… more connected to the inside… And one… settling… into a comfortable depth… where learning happens easily. And it’s interesting… because when you are in this state… You don’t have to believe anything. You don’t have to agree with anything. Your unconscious mind will simply take what is useful… And leave the rest. And that’s exactly what it does best.

You might already be aware… that thoughts have a habit of wandering. Drifting. Moving from one idea to another… like clouds crossing the sky. Some clouds are light. Some are heavy. Some are barely noticed. And yet… none of them stay forever. And it’s interesting… because many people believe they must control their thoughts.

But the truth is… Thoughts don’t need controlling. They need context. Because when you stop grabbing clouds… they drift. And when you stop feeding certain thoughts… they lose interest. And you may begin to realise… that many of your old thinking patterns… were never truths. They were habits.

And habits… are simply learned. And what is learned… can be gently unlearned. Not with force. But with experience. And you are having one now. And somewhere inside you… there is a part that already knows… how to think in ways that support you. That part existed before doubt.

Before self-criticism. Before overthinking. It never left. It simply became quieter. And now… it is being heard again. And when it is heard… it grows. And when it grows… it guides. And when it guides… you follow. Not because you must. But because it feels right. And positive thinking…

is not about pretending everything is perfect. It’s about seeing clearly. It’s about choosing what you give your attention to. Because attention… is like sunlight. Whatever it touches… grows. And perhaps you’re beginning to notice… that some thoughts don’t need your attention anymore. They don’t deserve it. Not because they’re wrong…

But because they’re no longer useful. And your unconscious mind… is very good at letting go of what isn’t useful. Just like trees drop leaves… when the season changes. They don’t fight the process. They respond to it. And you are in a new season now. A season of lighter thinking.

Softer interpretations. Kinder inner language. And you don’t have to make that happen. It is happening. Now. And you may begin to notice… that when you stop trying to be positive… something else happens. You become lighter. You become kinder. You become more patient with yourself. And those qualities naturally generate more pleasant thoughts.

Not because you demand them. But because they arise from safety. And when the nervous system feels safe… the mind becomes curious. And when the mind becomes curious… it opens. And open minds… don’t cling. They explore. And perhaps now… you can imagine opening a window in a room that’s been closed for a long time.

Not because anything was wrong… But because fresh air feels good. And the moment that window opens… air begins to move. Stagnant air doesn’t argue. It simply leaves. And fresh air arrives. And you don’t have to force it. You just open the window. And this process… is happening inside you now.

Old interpretations… gently drifting out. Fresh, lighter ones… quietly arriving. And you may notice… that some of your thoughts feel less important than they once did. Not wrong. Just… less interesting. And the mind… when bored… moves on. Which is why resistance isn’t necessary. Indifference is enough. And you may find…

that when a heavy thought appears… something in you says, “Oh. That again.” And it passes. Not because you fought it. But because you didn’t feed it. And what isn’t fed… weakens. What is nourished… grows. And you are nourishing gentler perspectives now. Small ones. Simple ones. And those are the most powerful.

Because they’re believable. They don’t argue. They don’t demand. They just make sense. And the unconscious mind loves what makes sense. And it integrates it. Automatically. And perhaps now… your mind is drifting… in that way… between thoughts… between awareness… between something and nothing. And that space… is fertile.

That space… is creative. That space… is where new habits form. And positivity… is simply a habit of noticing. Noticing what’s okay. Noticing what’s working. Noticing what’s here. And this noticing becomes automatic. And when it becomes automatic… it becomes effortless. And when it becomes effortless… it becomes identity.

And identity… shapes reality. And you may begin to sense now… that you are already different. Not dramatically. But subtly. And subtle shifts… are the ones that last. Because they integrate. They don’t clash. They don’t demand. They simply… become. And perhaps now… you can imagine a future moment. Not a special day. Just a normal one. And something happens… that once would have pulled you into heaviness. And you notice… that you respond differently. More calmly. More gently. More kindly. And you don’t think about it. Because it’s just how you are now.

And later… you realise. And you smile. And that smile… feels natural. Not forced. Not intentional. Just… there. And this will happen. In your own way. In your own time. Because your unconscious mind already knows how. And it is already doing it. Now. And in a moment… I will begin to count from one up to five. And with each number… you can gently return. Bringing with you… all the useful changes. All the lightness. All the softness. All the ease. One… becoming aware of your body again… Two… noticing the room around you…
Three… feeling refreshed… calm… clear… Four… gently more alert… And five… eyes open when ready… feeling lighter… clearer… and naturally more positive.

name: Self-Recognition & Confidence
use: Low confidence, doubting your abilities, or wanting to recognise your strengths and worth.

steps.text: It is just like setting out on a journey … knowing that you have prepared well … everything is packed … passports and tickets are in a safe place … all arrangements have been attended to … knowing that you can and you will complete your journey easily and without effort …

you have all the ability and the capability to do what you want … to make new and good things happen … you tell yourself now … I can … I will and you feel at ease … comfortable with yourself. What has happened in the past has happened …

from these events you have learned so much … experiences that will help you to know what is right for you … so you can relax and you can let go of all negative feelings and emotions … turning things around now and seeing the positive in everything.

For you … the glass is always half full as you refuse to accept that you have been guilty of failure … recognising with clarity that what you did was to do your very best and no-one can ask more than that … negative thoughts and emotions have no place at all in your life …

they hold you back …they prevent you from being that person that you wish to be and that you really can be. With every day you have learned more through experience … the best and most effective way of learning that can be … you are the product of all your experiences …

all of those events that occurred way back then ... you are better for your experiences as you continue to learn more each day about that wonderful ability that you do have to use all learnings in a positive and forward-looking way that tells you inside your own perfect inner mind, …

"I can … I will". You look back now to those events of childhood and of growing up which have shaped you and made you the person that you are … you take pride now in yourself as you recognise with ever increasing clarity of thought that you can be selective in those memories that you choose to keep …

to place value upon … for is it not true that everything has a value … and for you that is the value that you alone attribute to it? … Those things … those experiences that have no value to you … you reject now in their entirety …

you cast them away as over and done with. As you take control of your life … your emotions … things begin to happen the way you want them to happen … your clarity of thought and your ever increasing maturity ensure that you have all that you need … an attitude of mind that is positive and forward looking. As you move forward with your new attitudes … new confidence in your own ability … others around you will notice you and recognise that they can draw from you …

that strength comes from the knowledge that you can be relied on to act in a positive manner that overcomes difficulties … in a calm and ordered manner … and you begin to enjoy being valued … your contribution to all situations … your opinion sought as you grow and mature into that person that you want to be.

You have all that you need to be successful and you can be more relaxed and more comfortable in all situations … positive thinking can become a habit for you …for habits are created through repetition … the more you employ your positive attitudes of mind the stronger that habit can grow …

you practise being positive and confident … and the strangest thing happens … as you act positively and confidently … then you are seen to be positive … confident … it is as easy as that. Yours is that ability to choose how you wish to see …

with maturity and great pride in knowing that you no longer are that person caught in a trap of negative thinking … measuring all things in the light of what might go wrong … for you there is no failure … for you it does not exist as you recognise that the only measure that you need is that of your own success …

each grain of success is the foundation upon which all success is built … and this success is now your way … now and in the future … as you recognise that fear is just concern that you might lose control … and that is not true at all …

for to lose control … first you must have control … and you choose not to lose the ability that you know is yours to make things happen your way … "I can … I will" … these words now become an integral part of you … part of that internal harmony and peace of spirit that is now yours as you choose to take it and you will take it … will you not?

name: Releasing Insecurity
use: Feeling insecure, not good enough, self-doubt, or comparing yourself to others.

steps.text: Begin now by allowing yourself to become comfortable. You might be sitting or lying down… Whatever feels most supportive for you right now. Close your eyes gently. And bring your awareness to the breath…Noticing it…Not changing it… Just observing. Feel how each breath comes and goes without effort.

You don't need to force it. You don’t need to control it. Breathing happens all on its own… A perfect rhythm… quiet, calm… Steady. Allow the breath to lead you inward… Each inhale drawing you gently into yourself… Each exhale helping you let go… Letting go of the day…

Letting go of the thoughts… Letting go of the need to be anything or do anything right now. This is your time… To rest…To heal…To reconnect. And as you breathe…Begin to feel your body softening…Let the shoulders drop…Let the arms relax…Let the muscles of the face loosen…Let the chest open and the belly soften…

There’s nowhere to be…Nothing to fix…Nothing to figure out…You are safe here…You are supported…And you are already beginning to go deeper… In your mind now, imagine yourself standing at the edge of a quiet path…This is a path that leads inward…Not a place on a map, just a journey into your own self.

Take the first step. Feel how soft the earth is beneath your feet. There’s a gentle breeze… the kind that brushes against your skin and makes everything feel still… and alive. As you walk, you feel lighter… As if with each step, you're shedding something invisible— layers of tension, old worries, judgments that never truly belonged to you.

You don’t have to hold those anymore. They were just borrowed weights… and soon it will be time to put all of them down. With every step, feel yourself sinking deeper into calm… Deeper into quiet… Deeper into a kind of stillness that feels completely natural…like returning somewhere you’ve always known.

Ahead, you see a small clearing—peaceful, private… A sanctuary. Step into this space. And in the center of this space is a still pool. Crystal clear. Silent. It reflects everything honestly… without distortion… without judgment… Take a seat beside it now. This is the place where old things can be seen…

and gently released. As you sit by the pool, its surface begins to shift… And you begin to see images… Reflections of the past… Times when you felt small. Times when you doubted yourself. Times when fear convinced you that you weren’t enough. Just allow the images to come.

There’s no need to relive them— only to witness. They may be brief, fragmented, even unclear…And that’s okay. You don’t need to fix the past. You don’t need to carry it anymore either. You can simply acknowledge the following: “I see you. I remember. But I do not need you anymore.” And as each image appears, watch it begin to dissolve in the water…

as if the pool is gently absorbing each one… neutralizing them… returning them to the earth. And notice this now: Even as the reflections fade, you still remain. Clear. Whole. Present. Because who you truly are… has never been those insecurities. You are the one who witnessed them.

You are the space behind the thoughts. You are what remains when everything false falls away. Now, imagine that every insecurity you’ve carried— every “not good enough,” every “too much” or “not enough”— exists as a weight you’ve been carrying. Maybe in a bag over your shoulders… Maybe as tension in your body…

Maybe as thoughts that whisper when things get quiet. And now… Let yourself take that weight off. Physically imagine removing it. Feel the difference in your posture… in your breath… in your sense of self. They were not you. They were never you. They were old impressions, absorbed from others— From comparison, misunderstanding, fear…

And now, you are free to let them go. Say gently to yourself now: > “I let go of what was never mine. I release what no longer serves me. I no longer carry what I have outgrown.” And as those words settle in, you might notice a quiet shift…

A sense of space opening up inside you. As if some room has been cleared… for something more honest… more alive… more you. Now, in the center of this peaceful clearing, you notice something glowing softly. A light… warm, calm, steady. Not blinding… not loud. Just present. Always there.

And you realize: this is you. The you that existed before insecurity…The you untouched by fear or doubt…The you that remembers. This light is not something you have to create— It’s something you return to. Step into this light now. Let it fill you, surround you, infuse you…

Now listen as these truths are spoken gently within you: > “I am not broken. I am becoming.” > “I don’t need to push to be worthy. I am enough, even in stillness.” > “I let go of resistance. I move forward with ease.” Let these words become part of you now.

Let them anchor deep into your being. Softly. Gently. Naturally. No striving. No force. Just remembering. You are safe to be who you are…You are free to grow into all that you are becoming…There is no need to rush…There is no need to perform…The strongest trees grow quietly…The deepest waters move slowly…You are growing still…You are deepening still…And you are already whole.

Integration: A New Way of Moving Through Life Imagine now a version of yourself— not a fantasy, not a mask— but the truest you. The one who walks into each day without shrinking. The one who speaks without apology. The one who doesn’t chase validation… because they no longer question their worth.

See this version of you… Feel how they carry themselves. There’s a calm about them. An ease…A quiet certainty…Not loud…Not forceful….Just real. From this moment forward, this is how you begin to move through the world. With less effort…With more presence…With a quiet, growing trust in yourself. Insecurity may still whisper at times— but you don’t follow it anymore.

You don’t believe it like you used to. Because now, you know better. You’ve come home to yourself. And now, let these words settle into the most receptive part of your mind… the part that grows things from within… Let them drift in like seeds on the wind… I trust the pace and path of my own life. I let go of the need to prove myself. I release comparison and return to presence. I move through life with quiet confidence. I speak honestly and live gently.

I no longer shrink to fit. I no longer wait for permission. I am becoming who I already am. I am already enough. Take a moment to let those words echo through your mind… Feel them become yours… Not as something you’re trying to believe— But as something you’re beginning to remember.

In a moment, I’ll count from 1 to 5. And with each number, you’ll begin to return— bringing with you everything you’ve received today: the peace, the knowing, the soft strength… Not as something you need to hold tightly— but as something that now lives inside you. 1…

gently returning to the body… feeling supported, safe, grounded… 2… beginning to notice the breath again… bringing in more energy with each inhale… 3… wiggling the fingers and toes… bringing small movements back into the body… 4… returning to the space around you… feeling refreshed… present… quietly strong…
And 5… eyes open when you’re ready… awake, whole, and deeply at ease.

name: Releasing Fear & Remembering Your Worth
use: Held back by fear, feeling unworthy, or needing to reconnect with your own value.

steps.text: And as you settle into the place where you are right now… Perhaps noticing the way the air feels on your skin… The way your body begins to rest in its own weight… There’s no need to hurry… no need to try… Because the simple act of noticing your breath…

In and out… Rising and falling… Is enough to begin this gentle journey… You may become aware of the sound of your own breathing… The quiet rhythm… As if each breath is a wave, flowing in… and flowing out… And with each exhale, a little more of the day drifts away…

Like leaves carried downstream… And perhaps your eyes want to close now… Or perhaps they already have… Either way, you can let them… Because with each gentle blink, or with each moment they rest shut… Your mind finds it easier… To drift inward… Into that space where thoughts slow…

Where feelings can be felt without effort… Where you can simply be… And as you hear my voice… It’s almost as if it can float alongside your own inner voice… Blending together… Like two soft currents flowing into one river… Guiding you deeper and deeper… You may imagine a path now…

It begins right here where you are… And it winds ahead through a quiet, open landscape… Perhaps there are tall grasses swaying… Perhaps a warm, golden light rests upon them… Or perhaps the air is cool and clear… However it is for you… is exactly right… As you take the first step along that path…

Notice how each step feels easier than the one before… Your shoulders perhaps settling lower… Your breath naturally deepening… And there may be a sound, far off, like the gentle trickle of water… Or the hush of a soft breeze… The path itself seems to know where it is going…

You don’t need to decide… Your feet simply follow… As if they’ve always known the way… And as you walk… You begin to notice that this place seems to welcome you… Without question… Without judgement… It makes no demand on you to be anything other than who you are…

In this moment… Right now… And now you notice, up ahead, a small clearing… Sunlight spills across one side… While the other rests in soft, cool shade… Here, light and shadow meet… Each giving the other its shape… Perhaps you remember… How, as a child, there were times you felt the bright warmth of being seen and loved…

And other times… The cooler, heavier shadow of fear or doubt… And yet… here… in this clearing… Both light and shade belong… Each makes the other complete… You step into the sunlight for a moment… Feeling it touch your skin, gentle and warm… And then you step into the shade…

Cool, calm, soothing… Neither is wrong… Neither is all there is… Both are part of the same whole… And maybe there’s a deep relief in knowing… That you are not only the light moments of your life… And not only the shadow moments either… You are the whole landscape…

The open clearing that can hold it all… And as you stand there… You notice something… A small figure, just ahead, sitting by the base of a tree… It’s you… A younger you… Perhaps at an age when the world still felt uncertain… When fear sometimes curled in the belly…

When feeling worthy felt far away… You see the way this younger you sits… The way their shoulders might slope… The way their eyes look at the ground… And in this moment… You know that you can approach gently… As you come closer, you see that they sense you…

And they look up… Their eyes meeting yours… And perhaps they recognize you… Perhaps they feel something they can’t quite name… But there is a quiet safety in your presence… You kneel beside them… And without words at first… You simply allow them to feel that you are here…

That you are not going away… That they can be exactly as they are in this moment… And still be worthy of love… Nearby, you notice a river… Its surface smooth and slow in some places… Quick and dancing in others… And you invite the younger you to come with you to the riverbank…

You both stand at the edge… And you explain in the simplest way… That this river knows how to carry away old burdens… That anything too heavy to hold can be placed here… To drift downstream… The younger you may place in the water the fear they carried…

The feeling of not being enough… Perhaps they imagine these as small stones… Or as leaves… Or as threads… Whatever form they take… They are released into the water… And the current… without effort… Carries them away… Not to be fought… Not to be chased… Simply carried… You notice the way their shoulders lighten… The way their face begins to soften… And you may realize… That these old feelings never truly belonged to them… They were given, by the world… But they were never the truth… Beyond the river is a small garden… Untended in the way nature tends itself…

Wild flowers blooming among tall grasses… Trees growing at their own pace… Each plant different… And yet… All perfectly in place… You and your younger self walk there… And you both begin to see… That no tree asks the flower to be taller… No stream asks the rock to be smoother… Everything grows as it grows… And in that… Everything belongs… And perhaps there is a feeling… That you, too, have always been growing in your own way…

That even in times that felt still… Something in you was quietly unfolding… Becoming… Moving toward what you are now… The younger you seems to understand… Their eyes brighter… Their chest lifting with an easier breath… And you take a moment… To simply be here with them… Both of you knowing…

That worthiness is not earned… It is the soil you have always grown from… You notice a gentle breeze now… It moves through the garden… Through you… Through your younger self… And it feels as though it carries a message… Something wordless… Something true… That you have always been enough… That nothing in your past can take away what you are…

You and your younger self walk back toward the clearing… The path home appearing again before you… And before they return to wherever they belong within you… You hold them… You let them feel the warmth of your arms… The steadiness of your heart… And you say, perhaps silently…

“You are safe now. You are worthy. You are loved.” They smile… And then gently, they step into you… As if they’ve always been part of you… And now… they are home… And as you begin to return along the path… You may notice the way your body feels now…

Lighter… Freer… Stronger… With every step, you return more fully to the present moment… Bringing with you all the safety, all the worth, all the quiet strength you’ve touched here… The path becomes the place you are sitting or lying in now… The sounds around you return… Your breath… Your body… And when you are ready… You can open your eyes… Carrying with you… A deep knowing… That you are whole… And have always been whole.

name: Self-Esteem After a Break-Up
use: Hurting after a break-up or relationship ending, heartbreak, or rebuilding self-worth after rejection.

steps.text: Take a moment now to settle yourself… finding a comfortable position where your body can begin to rest… and your mind can begin to soften… And as you gently allow your eyes to close… or simply let your gaze drift… you may begin to notice your breathing… just as it is…

without needing to change anything at all… Because there is nothing to force… nothing to strive for… only a quiet allowing… a gentle permission to begin. And perhaps you can notice the rhythm of your breath… the natural rise… and fall… like waves that come in… and drift away again…

And with each breath out… you may begin to let go… just a little more… Letting go of the day… Letting go of effort… Letting go of anything that no longer needs your attention right now… And as your body begins to settle… you may notice a softening…

perhaps in the shoulders… or the jaw… or around the eyes… And you can allow that softness to spread… gently… naturally… Because your mind already knows how to relax… Your body already knows how to release… You don’t need to figure it out… only allow it. And as you continue breathing…

easily… comfortably… you might begin to imagine a place… a space that feels safe… calm… and completely your own… It may be a real place… or somewhere imagined… it doesn’t matter… Only that here… in this place… you are completely at ease… Perhaps there is a gentle warmth in the air…

A feeling of stillness… A quiet sense of being held… supported… And in this place… there is no judgement… No expectation… No need to be anything other than exactly who you are… Just you… as you are… and that is enough. And as you settle deeper into this space…

your mind can begin to drift inward… toward a deeper awareness… a deeper knowing… Because somewhere within you… there has always been a steady core… a part of you untouched by change… untouched by circumstance… untouched by what others say… or think… A part of you that knows your worth…

completely… quietly… and with certainty. And perhaps… over time… that voice has become quieter… overshadowed by other voices… Voices of doubt… Voices shaped by other people’s opinions… Moments where you were seen… or not seen… valued… or not valued… And experiences that may have left you questioning yourself…

But those experiences… no matter how significant they felt… were never a measure of your true value… They were moments… situations… interactions… Not definitions. And as you rest here… you can begin to gently separate… what happened… from who you are… Because who you are… goes deeper than any relationship…

Deeper than any role… Deeper than anyone else’s perception… And just for a moment… imagine placing those external opinions… those past moments… gently to one side… Not pushing them away… not resisting them… just setting them down… And as you do… notice what remains… Because underneath all of that…

there is still you… Whole… Intact… And inherently worthy. And you may begin to sense… even faintly… that your worth was never something given to you by another person… And it was never something that could be taken away. It has always been there… Quietly steady… like a foundation beneath everything else.

And perhaps now… you can begin to reconnect with that foundation… Not through effort… But through remembering… Because there have been times in your life… however small… where you felt a sense of confidence… A sense of ease in yourself… A sense of simply being enough… And those moments matter…

because they came from within you… Not from approval… Not from validation… But from your own internal state. And that means they can return… grow… and expand again. Because your mind is capable of change… Your perspective is capable of shifting… And your sense of self can be rebuilt…

stronger… clearer… and more grounded than ever before. And now… I’d like you to imagine a gentle light… somewhere within you… Perhaps in your chest… or your centre… A soft… steady glow… This light represents your inherent worth… your value… your identity beyond roles… beyond labels… And with each breath in…

this light begins to grow a little brighter… And with each breath out… it spreads… gently through your body… Filling your chest… Your shoulders… Your arms… Your stomach… Your legs… A quiet… steady expansion of something that has always been there… And as this light grows… it begins to dissolve old patterns…

old beliefs that were never truly yours… Beliefs like… “I’m not enough” Or “I need others to validate me” Or “My worth depends on being chosen” And as these beliefs soften… they begin to lose their hold… Because they were learned… not true. And in their place… something new begins to form…

A deeper understanding… that your worth is constant… Independent… Unshakeable… And as this new understanding settles in… you may begin to feel a subtle shift… A sense of grounding… A quiet confidence… Not loud or forced… but steady… calm… and real. And from this place… you can begin to see your situation differently…
