Not as a reflection of your value… But as a chapter… an experience… a turning point… Because relationships can end… not because someone lacks worth… But because paths change… needs differ… growth moves in different directions… And that says nothing about your value as a person. And as you allow that idea to settle…

you may begin to feel a sense of release… A loosening of the weight you may have been carrying… Because you don’t need to carry someone else’s choices as a judgement of yourself… You can set that down now. And in its place… you can begin to build something new…

A relationship with yourself… based on respect… understanding… and compassion… And you may begin to notice… a quiet sense of possibility emerging… Because when your worth is no longer tied to others… you become free… Free to grow… Free to explore… Free to become who you are meant to be…

And there is so much potential within you… Potential that may have been hidden… or overlooked… but never lost… And now… it can begin to re-emerge… In small ways at first… Moments of clarity… Moments of strength… Moments where you choose yourself… gently… naturally… And each of these moments builds…

Strengthening new pathways… new beliefs… Until confidence begins to feel more familiar… Self-respect begins to feel natural… And self-worth becomes something you simply know… Rather than something you question. And as this process continues… you may begin to feel a growing sense of optimism… Not forced… not unrealistic…

But grounded… steady… and real. Because your future is not defined by your past… It is shaped by what you choose… what you believe… and how you see yourself moving forward… And now… you can begin to see a future where you feel stronger… More centred… More certain…

More connected to who you truly are… A future where you trust yourself… Where you value yourself… Where you no longer seek permission to feel worthy… Because you already are. And as you imagine that version of yourself… notice how she carries herself… How she moves… How she thinks…

How she responds to the world… There is a calm strength there… A quiet assurance… A sense of inner stability… And that version of you… is not separate from you… She is you… emerging. And step by step… breath by breath… you are moving closer to that way of being…

Naturally… effortlessly… in your own time. And now… just take a moment to let all of this settle… Allowing these ideas… these feelings… to integrate in whatever way is right for you… Because your mind knows what to do with them… And your unconscious mind can continue this process…

even after this session ends… Strengthening your sense of self… Releasing what no longer serves you… And building a deeper… more stable sense of worth… From within. And as we begin to gently return… you can carry this feeling with you… This sense of grounding… This quiet confidence…

This growing awareness of your true value… And you may find… in the coming days… that you respond differently… Thinking differently… Feeling differently… Seeing yourself more clearly… And each of these changes… however small… is a step forward. So now… begin to bring your awareness back to your breath…

Back to your body… Noticing the surface beneath you… The space around you… And when you’re ready… gently begin to move your fingers… your toes… And in your own time… allowing your eyes to open… Feeling calm… centred… and just a little more connected to your true self…
And knowing… quietly… deeply… That your worth has always been there… And it always will be.

name: Overcoming Cravings & Addiction
use: Struggling with cravings, urges, or wanting to break free from a substance or habit.

steps.text: Take a moment now to find a comfortable position. You may sit or lie down, whichever feels right for you… and as you settle in, just notice the surface supporting you. Allow your eyes to gently close, if they haven’t already… and begin to notice the natural rhythm of your breathing.

No need to change it… simply notice. Breathing in… and out… letting the breath guide you inward. Each breath is like a gentle wave, flowing in… and out… and with every out-breath, you can allow a little more release, a little more letting go. Notice your shoulders softening…

your jaw unclenching… your hands becoming loose and relaxed. That’s right… and as your body begins to soften and relax, your mind can begin to drift inward. Imagine with each exhale, you are releasing tension… releasing stress… releasing old patterns that no longer serve you. And with each inhale, you are drawing in peace…

strength… clarity. You may already notice a sense of heaviness in the eyelids, a lightness in the forehead… and you might even feel as though you are drifting, floating gently deeper into comfort. Now imagine you are standing at the top of a beautiful staircase. It may be made of wood, or stone, or marble…

whatever comes to mind is just right. And this staircase leads to a place of deep inner wisdom… a place of healing and renewal. In a moment, I’ll invite you to step down… and with each step, you’ll double your sense of calm and relaxation. Take the first step down now…

10… deeper still. 9… letting go. 8… floating down. 7… peace surrounding you. 6… calm expanding. 5… halfway down now, safe, supported. 4… even deeper. 3… every step is healing. 2… almost there. 1… arriving at a deep, safe, healing place. Good. And here in this place, your subconscious is listening closely, ready to receive new directions, new freedom, new possibilities.

Addiction, in the past, may have felt powerful. It may have seemed like it had control over you. And yet, deep inside, there is a greater power. A greater strength. A wisdom and clarity that is yours. And today, you are reconnecting with that strength. That freedom. That natural ability to choose.

Because addiction is not who you are. It is not your identity. It is something you experienced… but it is not you. You are far more than any habit, far more than any craving, far more than any temporary urge. As you sit here, deeply relaxed, you can imagine the craving as a cloud in the sky.

It drifts in… and it drifts out. It is not permanent. It cannot control the sky. And you are the sky. Vast. Expansive. Unchanged by passing clouds. Notice now how easily the cloud dissolves, as the sun of your inner strength shines through. That strength is always there.

Even when the clouds appear, the strength of the sun remains constant. And you can remind yourself, anytime you notice an urge, that it is temporary. It will pass. And each time you let it pass, you grow stronger, more free, more in control. Your subconscious can now understand that addiction was once an attempt.

An attempt to soothe, to comfort, to escape, to provide something you felt you needed. But now you know… there are healthier, wiser, more empowering ways to meet those needs. The old behavior is no longer necessary. It has no real power over you. It was only a false solution.

And now, you are ready for true solutions. Your body is ready for health. Your mind is ready for clarity. Your heart is ready for freedom. And every time in the future when the old urge appears, your mind will automatically pause. You will take a breath. And in that breath, you will remember: You are stronger than the urge.

You are wiser than the craving. You are free. And in that pause, you will choose differently. You will choose health. You will choose life. You will choose yourself. Imagine yourself now, one week from today. Notice how much lighter you feel. Notice how empowered you are. You wake up with energy, with clarity.

You smile, knowing you are in control. Now imagine a month from today. See yourself walking with confidence, your body stronger, your mind clearer, your spirit freer than ever before. Now imagine six months from today. You look back and realize how far you’ve come. The old urges feel distant, like a story that no longer belongs to you.

You hardly even think about it anymore, because you are too busy living fully, loving deeply, thriving in every way. And imagine a year from now. See yourself healthy, vibrant, free. Your relationships stronger, your sense of self renewed. And you look back and say: “I am free.

I did it. I am stronger than the addiction. And I will never go back.” Imagine now that you are holding a chain in your hands. This chain represents the old addiction. Heavy, restricting, binding. And as you hold it, you notice something remarkable. The links are weak.

They are brittle. They crumble like dust in your hands. And as the chain falls apart, you feel lighter. You feel free. You realize the chain was never as strong as you thought. The real strength has always been in you. Now, standing tall, you take a deep breath.

And as you exhale, you feel that freedom radiating through every part of your being. From this moment forward, your subconscious mind accepts these truths: You are free. You are stronger than any craving. You no longer need the old habit. You choose health, life, freedom. Every day you grow stronger, healthier, freer.

And each time you hear yourself think, “I can’t,” your subconscious will immediately remind you: “Yes, I can. I already am.” And every time you take a deep breath instead of giving in, your brain rewires itself. The old pathways weaken. The new pathways strengthen. Until freedom becomes effortless, natural, automatic.

Now imagine stepping into a healing light. This light surrounds you with warmth, with comfort, with renewal. It flows into every cell of your body, washing away toxins, washing away old patterns, bringing fresh energy, fresh life. You can feel your organs repairing, your lungs clearing, your skin glowing, your mind sharpening, your heart strengthening.

Every part of you is healing, restoring, renewing. And as this light fills you, you realize you are whole. You are complete. You are free. Place your hand now over your heart. Feel the steady rhythm within you. This heartbeat is life. This heartbeat is strength. This heartbeat is freedom.

And from now on, whenever you place your hand over your heart, your subconscious will remember this moment. It will remember your freedom. It will remind you of your strength. And you will feel empowered, clear, calm. In a moment, I’ll count from 1 to 5. With each number, you’ll begin to return to full awareness.

Bringing with you all the strength, freedom, and clarity you’ve connected with today. 1… becoming aware of your body again. 2… feeling energy return to your muscles. 3… taking a deep breath, feeling refreshed. 4… wiggling fingers and toes, eyes becoming lighter. 5… eyes open, fully alert, feeling calm, strong, free.
Take a moment now to notice how you feel. Notice the strength within you. Notice the freedom already unfolding. Because the truth is, you are free. You are strong. And this is only the beginning.
